songs.name: White & Nerdy (Parody of "Ridin'" by Chamillionaire feat. Krayzie Bone)

voters.name: Nate
voters.comment: More "hip" parents. I would never think to listen to weird al with my dad, though he'd probably like it.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I think I can imagine a few of our dads qualifying for "White & Nerdy"

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I love that your parents like Weird Al!

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I like it but I heard it a lot.

voters.name: Dorothy
voters.comment: You have cooler parents than I did lol

voters.name: Nate
voters.comment: I don't really agree the message, but it's so much better than blurred lines

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: As a former ELA teacher, and also a big hater of the original song, I'm morally obliged to give this song a point!

voters.name: Caleb
voters.comment: I like this one. I like Weird Al but I liked other ones that I think were more deserving of redemption.

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Thank god we've got some Weird Al in here now

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: Totally counts! I forgot this version existed and I considered submitting Lola so when this started playing I was like "Oh cool someone submitted Lola" and then I was in for a surprise 😂

voters.name: Mike
voters.comment: A parody song with a name as the title, parodying a song with a name as the title. It almost double counts.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: I was wondering when we'd get a Weird Al submission! As a notorious Star Wars disliker, I think I need to skip voting for this song in particular. But I do love me some Weird Al

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I considered this one, and now it keeps getting stuck in my head so imma give it 2 votes

voters.name: Saa
voters.comment: Listening to this on TDOV hit a little different. <3 Still a banger.

voters.name: Lia
voters.comment: I almost submitted a Lady Gaga song in the "get turnt" round! She's had so many good bops over the years, this isn't one of my favorite just because I feel like it's been overplayed BUT a great message of course
